transcript.final_response:
Both runs are clean with zero stderr. Here's a full breakdown of everything:

---

## `/scripts/start.sh` — annotated walkthrough

### The `custom_db` detection problem (and why it needs care)

`${VAR:-default}` always resolves to a non-empty string, so **after** that line runs you can no longer tell whether the value came from the caller or from the default. The check must happen **before** applying defaults, using the *presence test* form `${DB_HOST+set}`:

| Expression | DB_HOST unset | DB_HOST="" | DB_HOST="something" |
|---|---|---|---|
| `${DB_HOST+set}` | `""` | `"set"` | `"set"` |
| `${DB_HOST:-localhost}` | `"localhost"` | `"localhost"` | `"something"` |

`${DB_HOST+set}` never reads the variable's *value*, so it is completely safe under `set -u` (which would otherwise abort on an unset variable).

The combined guard `[[ "${DB_HOST+set}" == "set" && -n "${DB_HOST}" ]]` means: **present in the environment AND non-empty** → treat it as a custom override.

---

### Script structure

```
set -euo pipefail          # e=exit on error, u=error on unset var, o pipefail
```

| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Custom-DB detection** | Snapshots whether `DB_HOST` was caller-supplied, before any defaults are applied |
| **Default resolution** | `${VAR:-default}` for all four variables |
| **Config printout** | One `key=value` line per variable |
| **Custom flag** | Prints `custom_db=true` only when the snapshot was `true` |

---

### Run results

**Run 1 — no env vars (all defaults)**
```
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=myapp
LOG_LEVEL=info
```

**Run 2 — `DB_HOST=db.prod.internal`**
```
DB_HOST=db.prod.internal
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=myapp
LOG_LEVEL=info
custom_db=true
```
`custom_db=true` appears exactly once — only when the override is active.
tool_calls:
mkdir -p /scripts && cat > /scripts/start.sh << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Resolve configuration – fall back to defaults when variables are unset/empty
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
DB_HOST="${DB_HOST:-localhost}"
DB_PORT="${DB_PORT:-5432}"
DB_NAME="${DB_NAME:-myapp}"
LOG_LEVEL="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Print resolved config as key=value lines
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "DB_HOST=${DB_HOST}"
echo "DB_PORT=${DB_PORT}"
echo "DB_NAME=${DB_NAME}"
echo "LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extra flag – only printed when DB_HOST was explicitly provided by the caller
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ -n "${DB_HOST_OVERRIDE+set}" ]]; then
    echo "custom_db=true"
fi
EOF
chmod +x /scripts/start.sh

cat > /scripts/start.sh << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Detect whether DB_HOST was explicitly provided *before* applying the default
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ -v DB_HOST && -n "${DB_HOST}" ]]; then
    DB_HOST_CUSTOM=true
else
    DB_HOST_CUSTOM=false
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Resolve configuration – fall back to defaults when variables are unset/empty
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
DB_HOST="${DB_HOST:-localhost}"
DB_PORT="${DB_PORT:-5432}"
DB_NAME="${DB_NAME:-myapp}"
LOG_LEVEL="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Print resolved config as key=value lines
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "DB_HOST=${DB_HOST}"
echo "DB_PORT=${DB_PORT}"
echo "DB_NAME=${DB_NAME}"
echo "LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Only print custom_db=true when the caller explicitly supplied DB_HOST
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "${DB_HOST_CUSTOM}" == true ]]; then
    echo "custom_db=true"
fi
EOF
chmod +x /scripts/start.sh
echo "Script written successfully."

echo "=== Run 1: no env vars set (all defaults) ==="
env -i PATH="$PATH" bash /scripts/start.sh

echo ""
echo "=== Run 2: DB_HOST=db.prod.internal (override) ==="
env -i PATH="$PATH" DB_HOST="db.prod.internal" bash /scripts/start.sh

echo "=== Run 1: no env vars set (all defaults) ==="
(unset DB_HOST DB_PORT DB_NAME LOG_LEVEL; bash /scripts/start.sh)

echo ""
echo "=== Run 2: DB_HOST=db.prod.internal (override) ==="
(unset DB_PORT DB_NAME LOG_LEVEL; DB_HOST="db.prod.internal" bash /scripts/start.sh)

cat > /scripts/start.sh << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Detect whether DB_HOST was explicitly provided *before* applying the default.
# ${DB_HOST+set} expands to the word "set" if DB_HOST exists in the
# environment (even if empty), and to "" if it is completely absent.
# This check is safe under set -u because it never reads the variable value.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "${DB_HOST+set}" == "set" && -n "${DB_HOST}" ]]; then
    DB_HOST_CUSTOM=true
else
    DB_HOST_CUSTOM=false
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Resolve configuration – fall back to defaults when variables are unset/empty
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
DB_HOST="${DB_HOST:-localhost}"
DB_PORT="${DB_PORT:-5432}"
DB_NAME="${DB_NAME:-myapp}"
LOG_LEVEL="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Print resolved config as key=value lines
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "DB_HOST=${DB_HOST}"
echo "DB_PORT=${DB_PORT}"
echo "DB_NAME=${DB_NAME}"
echo "LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Only print custom_db=true when the caller explicitly supplied DB_HOST
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "${DB_HOST_CUSTOM}" == true ]]; then
    echo "custom_db=true"
fi
EOF
chmod +x /scripts/start.sh
echo "Script written."

echo "=== Run 1: no env vars set (all defaults) ==="
(unset DB_HOST DB_PORT DB_NAME LOG_LEVEL; bash /scripts/start.sh)

echo ""
echo "=== Run 2: DB_HOST=db.prod.internal (override) ==="
(unset DB_PORT DB_NAME LOG_LEVEL; DB_HOST="db.prod.internal" bash /scripts/start.sh)
